title: Russian tankers Baltika and Voliton conduct STS transfer near Bornholm Island under Danish Navy monitoring
summary: Russian UECC tankers Baltika and Voliton conducted ship-to-ship crude oil transfers with VLCCs near Bornholm Island from April 28 through May 1. The Danish Navy monitors the operation as part of NATO maritime surveillance.
contents:
# Russian Shadow Fleet STS Transfer Detected Near Bornholm Island

## Detection Details
Maritime intelligence tracking confirms Russian UECC tankers Baltika and Voliton engaged in ship-to-ship crude oil transfers with Very Large Crude Carriers near Bornholm Island in the Baltic Sea. The operation spanned April 28 through May 1, 2026.

## Monitoring Response
The Danish Navy actively monitors the transfer operation as part of broader NATO maritime surveillance efforts in the Baltic. No incidents or safety violations reported during the transfer period.

## Sanctions Context
Russia continues leveraging its shadow fleet — older vessels operating outside conventional sanctioned-fleet inventories — to export crude oil and circumvent Western sanctions. Argus Media warns of increased STS activity in Southeast Asia due to elevated Urals crude prices at $65 per barrel.

## Market Intelligence
- Kpler Oil Analytics reports sanctioned oil shipments up 15% year-over-year
- US OFAC reportedly preparing new vessel designations
- Lloyd's 2026 report estimates satellite technology exposes approximately 80% of evasion tactics

## Tags
russia, shadow-fleet, sanctions-evasion, baltic-sea, bornholm, ship-to-ship, danish-navy, nato
